hypothesis: E616 attributed the byte-identical E615 matched control/treatment eval (schema_role_slot_decode_weight=0 vs =8.0) to the OOD Gallery typed array closing empty before the E615 object-frame lever's precondition is ever reached (E612's already-rejected 'empty array close' finding, one level upstream). This iteration asks a further-upstream falsifiable question: does the E612-authored semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_margin_decode_weight lever (already active at weight 2.0 in the E611-E616 recipe, specifically meant to prevent that empty close) actually execute at all on this checkpoint class, or is it gated out by something in the recipe itself?
environment_note: Fresh container, empty outputs/ dir (same starting condition as E615/E616). torch installed via `uv sync --extra dev --extra torch`. Node/AgentV commands run with NODE_OPTIONS unset (ambient --import tsx breaks the openui_bridge subprocess and pytest's Node calls), matching the documented E615/E616 fix.
method: Retrained the identical E616 recipe (same corpus/seed/architecture, 80 scratch steps) to reproduce its checkpoint, then added temporary instrumentation (reverted before commit) inside _semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_bias to print which internal gate returns None on every call during the E616 control eval. All 111 calls into the array-decision branch failed the very first gate: 'not slot_contract' -- self._slot_contracts[row] was None. Traced this to _generate_batch_once (twotower.py): self._slot_contracts is only populated when config.slot_contract_constrained_decode or config.template_fill_decode is true; neither flag was ever passed by the E611-E616 eval CLI recipe (confirmed against all four surviving eval_recipe JSON blocks in docs/design/iter-e611.. through iter-e616..). schema_role_slot_decode_weight (_schema_role_slot_bias), slot_coverage_close_decode_weight (_slot_coverage_close_bias), semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_margin_decode_weight and semantic_plan_typed_array_item_margin_decode_weight (_semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_bias), and semantic_plan_repeated_slot_margin_decode_weight (_semantic_plan_repeated_slot_bias) all gate on the same `not slot_contract` check, so all five were silently no-ops in every E611-E616 eval regardless of their weight or the checkpoint's training depth. Re-ran the exact E616 recipe with --slot-contract-constrained-decode added (no other recipe values changed) against the same checkpoint SHA and confirmed the array-decision gate now passes and the array opens an item.

checkpoint.sync_reason: Local-only 80-step scratch diagnostic checkpoint (E540-E548/E615/E616 fallback style); --no-sync-checkpoints. sha256 is byte-identical to E616's checkpoint (same corpus/seed/steps/architecture, confirming this is a true replay, not a different model).

training.comparison_to_e616_checkpoint: Identical training recipe to E616 (same corpus/seed/steps/architecture); checkpoint sha256 matches E616's byte-for-byte, confirming this replays the same model rather than introducing a confound.

eval_recipe.slot_contract_constrained_decode_note: The single flag change vs. the E611-E616 recipe: this is what populates self._slot_contracts and un-gates the five decode weights below.
eval_recipe.matched_to: E614/E615/E616 recipe weights, replayed control (schema_role_slot_decode_weight=0) vs treatment (=8.0) on the same E616 checkpoint SHA, with --slot-contract-constrained-decode added

prediction_parity.note: All 4 of 4 OOD predictions differ between control and treatment, for the first time in the E611-E616 lineage on a same-checkpoint matched pair. schema_role_slot_decode_weight applies to both top-level component arguments (unchanged since E591) and E615's object-frame properties, so the dashboard/modal/auth deltas reflect the pre-existing top-level lever finally executing too, not just the E615 addition.

gallery_bug_check.target: Whether ImageGallery item src/alt each bind to their own matching visible slot instead of collapsing to one (E614's narrative bug), observed end-to-end for the first time.
gallery_bug_check.reason: With slot_contract_constrained_decode enabled, self._slot_contracts is populated and (a) the array now opens an item object instead of closing empty (semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_margin_decode_weight's own precondition is finally reachable), and (b) once inside the object, schema_role_slot_decode_weight=8.0 (E615's object-frame branch) steers src toward the img-role slot and alt toward the alt-role slot instead of both collapsing onto whichever slot scores highest overall (control's behavior, both properties on :ood.gallery.alt). This is the exact E614 narrative bug E615 targeted, now demonstrated end-to-end on a real checkpoint and a real eval, not just at the unit level.

root_cause.summary: Not a checkpoint-undertraining or decode-lever-absence problem. self._slot_contracts (twotower.py, _generate_batch_once) is only populated when config.slot_contract_constrained_decode or config.template_fill_decode is true. The E611-E616 eval CLI recipe set honest_slot_contract=true and nonzero weights for five slot-contract-gated decode biases (schema_role_slot_decode_weight, slot_coverage_close_decode_weight, semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_margin_decode_weight, semantic_plan_typed_array_item_margin_decode_weight, semantic_plan_repeated_slot_margin_decode_weight) but never passed slot_contract_constrained_decode or template_fill_decode. honest_slot_contract and slot_contract_constrained_decode are orthogonal flags (one governs the inventory *source*, honest prompt-only vs legacy gold fallback; the other governs whether the resolved inventory is actually plumbed into decode-time biases as self._slot_contracts) and the recipe conflated them. Every one of these five biases silently returned None on every decode step across E611, E612, E613, E614, E615, and E616, independent of weight value or checkpoint quality.
root_cause.fix_kind: recipe correction plus a fail-loud code guard (not a new decode-time lever, not more training)
root_cause.code_change: Added a ValueError in TwoTowerModel._generate_batch_once (src/slm_training/models/twotower.py) that raises when any of the five contract-gated decode weights is set >0 while slot_contract_constrained_decode and template_fill_decode are both false, naming the offending weight(s) and the fix. This prevents future recipes from silently repeating E611-E616's no-op pattern.

comparison_to_e616: E616 concluded the byte-identical result reproduced E612's already-rejected 'empty array close' finding one level upstream of the E615 lever. This iteration shows that conclusion was itself one level short: the array closing empty was a symptom of self._slot_contracts being None (a missing CLI flag), not of the E612 nonempty-margin lever failing on its own merits or the checkpoint being categorically too weak. With the flag corrected, the same 80-step scratch checkpoint from this session (byte-identical sha256 to E616's) opens a Gallery item and E615's fix is now observed end-to-end with a real, non-trivial, non-identical quality delta.
decision: Retain the E617 ValueError guard as permanent, default-on safety wiring (no config flag; it only fires when contract-gated weights are already set without their required companion). Retain E611-E616's code unchanged. Do not promote or ship this checkpoint: binding_aware_meaningful_v2_rate_strict remains 0.0 in both arms, meaningful_program_rate is unchanged at 0.5, and this is a single small (n=4) matched replay, not a confirmatory suite. The next real matched-pair session for any of these levers must include --slot-contract-constrained-decode (or --template-fill-decode); the new ValueError makes omitting it a hard failure instead of a silent no-op.
next: Re-run the E611 (repeated-plan slot allocation) and E614/E615 recipes with slot_contract_constrained_decode added on a real trained/warm-started checkpoint (not another 80-step scratch model) to see whether the same-direction, real deltas observed here (placeholder_fidelity +0.042, placeholder_validity +0.025, reward_score +0.013) hold or grow at scale, and whether binding_aware_meaningful_v2_rate_strict finally moves off 0.0 once slot-contract-gated decode is actually active.

ship_gates.reason: Strict v2 remains 0 in both arms; n=4 is diagnostic, not a confirmatory suite; checkpoint is an 80-step CPU scratch diagnostic, never synced.

verification.unit_tests: tests/test_models/test_compiler_decode.py: 97 passed (90 pre-existing + 7 new E617 parametrized tests: test_contract_gated_decode_weight_without_slot_contract_decode_raises x5, test_contract_gated_decode_weight_with_slot_contract_decode_does_not_raise x2). Run with NODE_OPTIONS unset (ambient --import tsx breaks the openui_bridge Node subprocess used by several tests, same fix documented in E615/E616).
verification.verify_version_stamps: python -m scripts.verify_version_stamps --check -> ok (3 changed files, 1 component touched: model.twotower v58->v59)

version_stamp.note: eval version_stamp reflects the registered component version at eval time (v58, code_dirty=true); versions.json was bumped to v59 for model.twotower as part of committing this iteration's twotower.py guard, and python -m scripts.verify_version_stamps --check confirms the bump matches the diff.
